title: CLI Authority Audit
goal: Implement #357 by auditing remaining hand-authored CLI authority across root, planning, and memory CLIs before generated adapter work.

intent.outcome: A compact audit identifies which CLI command surfaces are generation-ready, missing contracts, hand-authored authority, runtime-owned primitives, or deferred.
intent.constraints: Stay on master; keep this as an audit slice; do not start the #356 generation migration.
intent.latitude: Choose the artifact location and classification detail that best supports the next slice.
intent.escalation: Stop before implementing generated adapters or broad command refactors.

execution.next_step: Inspect CLI modules and contract sources, then write the compact audit artifact.
execution.proof: Use proof --changed, planning surface checks, reconcile, summary.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Generate shipped CLI adapters from authoritative package definitions instead of treating executable CLI code as interface authority.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After #357 audit is closed and a low-risk generated adapter candidate is selected.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Generated CLI adapter work can start from classified command authority instead of broad CLI rediscovery.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Root CLI command shape is already manifest-backed; planning and memory package CLIs still own interface authority directly in argparse.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: The audit classified root, planning, and memory CLI authority and identified root `defaults` as the first low-risk generated adapter candidate.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Run planning-surface and maintainer-surface validation after archive closeout.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: #358 should define the command-adapter generation contract, then #359 can start generated adapter work from the root `defaults` command.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Do whatever further work you can already now.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Continue current open product-compression/follow-up work by handling the next safe prerequisite slice.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check that this is an audit only and identifies the first generation candidate without implementing it.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: planning state/evidence/execplan/review artifacts and a compact audit artifact.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Any code generation or CLI behavior change.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: src/agentic_workspace/cli.py behavior, packages/* CLI behavior, contract schemas.

stop_conditions.stop when: #357 is closed with a compact audit and this plan archives cleanly.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The audit requires command behavior changes to classify accurately.

context_budget.live working set: issues #356/#357, root/planning/memory CLI modules, relevant command contracts, this execplan
context_budget.recoverable later: full CLI implementation details after audit
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run and closeout_distillation before archive.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before inspecting individual command implementations in depth beyond audit needs.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Close #357 with a compact CLI authority audit.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not implement generation or change CLI behavior.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Audit artifact format and first candidate recommendation.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The user expects #356 implementation rather than audit closure.

immediate_next_action: Inspect CLI and contract surfaces and write the audit artifact.
invariants: Do not add shipped CLI generation in this audit slice.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Runtime primitive implementation is not the same as command/interface authority.
open_questions_to_close: Closed: root `agentic-workspace defaults` is the first low-risk generated adapter candidate.
completion_criteria: Audit covers root, planning, and memory CLIs.
Audit identifies first generated adapter candidate.
No active execplan remains after archive closeout.

execution_run.handoff source: preflight, gh issue list, local CLI/contract inspection
execution_run.what happened: Promoted #357, inspected root/planning/memory CLI authority and root operation contracts, then wrote a compact review artifact.
execution_run.scope touched: Planning state, active execplan, and one review artifact.
execution_run.validations run: preflight, gh issue list, git status, local CLI/contract inspection.
execution_run.result for continuation: Use #358 to define the command-adapter generation contract, then #359 to generate or mechanically assemble the root `defaults` adapter first.
execution_run.next step: Archive this plan after closeout and validation.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; no CLI behavior or generated adapter implementation was changed
finished_run_review.proof status: proof selector chose planning_surfaces and maintainer_surfaces; final commands pending
finished_run_review.config compliance: stayed on master and used package planning/preflight surfaces

proof_report.validation proof: uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed ... --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; make maintainer-surfaces; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py
proof_report.proof achieved now: Proof selector returned planning_surfaces and maintainer_surfaces; planning doctor reported healthy; maintainer surfaces completed ok; planning-surface drift check reported no warnings.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: docs/reviews/cli-authority-audit-2026-04-26.md plus successful local validation command outputs.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Do whatever further work you can already now.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Reduce executable CLI code as an authority surface while preserving runtime primitives.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can the next generated adapter slice start without broad rediscovery?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #357 was a bounded audit prerequisite; generated adapter implementation remains open under #356.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen #357 if #358 or #359 cannot proceed without broad CLI authority rediscovery.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: CLI authority audit for root, planning, and memory CLIs.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Proof selector, planning doctor, maintainer surfaces, and planning-surface drift check passed.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: Review artifact records command classification and first candidate.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): Review artifact only; no startup surface added.
execution_summary.resume from: #358 command-adapter generation contract, then #359 root `defaults` generated adapter candidate.

discard.summary: Raw inspection details not needed after the audit artifact.

continuation.summary: #358 remains open to define the command-adapter generation contract before #359 implements root `defaults` generation.

issue_follow_up.summary: CLI authority audit complete; continue generated adapter work through #358, then #359, under #356.

drift_log: 2026-04-26: Promoted #357 as the next safe work after #355.
